title: Cloud Security Analyst
description: * Experience 5 years of progressive experience in Information Security with at least 3 years focused on Cloud security and 2 years on Kubernetes, Container security * Cloud Expertise Handson experience with at least two major public clouds GCP and Azure preferred AWS and OCI a plus * Container Security Strong expertise in Kubernetes security controls RBAC Secrets Ingress TLS and container runtime internals * Technical Skills Proficiency in scripting Python preferred Bash or Go a plus and working knowledge of Infrastructure as Code IaC * Compliance Frameworks Knowledge of information security standards eg ISO 27001 SOC 2 NIST and CIS Benchmarks for Cloud and Kubernetes * Professional Certifications CISSP CKS Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist or cloud specific certifications GCP Professional Cloud Security Engineer Azure Security Engineer are highly desirable * Soft Skills Proven ability to influence engineering teams without direct authority and experience working in multinational corporate environments

title: Company Secretary
description: Our client, a leading Law Firm based in Dublin 2 is looking for a fully qualified Company Secretary to join their large team. The role offers opportunities for advancement, good work life balance and an excellent package **Call Cormac Spencer now on 01 845 4651 for more info!** **Salary:** to €60k **Benefits:** Pension, Bonus, Gym, Canteen, Travel Tax Saver Scheme, 2 days WFH and more **Total Package:** up to €80k **Key Responsibilities:** * Ensure the maintenance of company secretarial records, including Minute Books and Statutory Registers. * To liaise with external stakeholders as well as being the point of contact for client companies and their board of directors. * To manage the proper handling of both corporate and financial transactions of clients. * To ensure all other requirements under the Companies Act 2014 are met. * Attend shareholder and board meetings, recording and circulating minutes. * To maintain and grow relationships with existing clients and intermediaries. * Monitor transaction of the companies under management in accordance with the transaction monitoring process and procedure. * Manage and coordinate the reporting of any legal issues with clients. * To ensure client files are in accordance with the filing and archiving processes and update models. * Assist with the training of Junior team members when required. **The Candidate:** * 3\+ years' experience as a Company Secretary. * Previous experience working in a corporate services provider/law firm. * Part / Fully CGI Qualified. * Experience attending board meetings and drafting minutes. * Ability to use own initiative and be adaptable. * Good attention to detail and very organised individual. **If this Company Secretary Role Sounds Like You, Call Cormac NOW on 01 845 4651\.** **Speak to the Experts with the Best Company Secretary Jobs in Ireland** **Link Personnel** **01 845 4651**

title: Consultant - Operation Analyst
description: At Turner \& Townsend we’re passionate about making the difference. That means delivering better outcomes for our clients, helping our people to realize their potential, and doing our part to create a prosperous society. Every day we help our major global clients deliver ambitious and highly technical projects, in over 130 countries worldwide. Our team is dynamic, innovative, and client\-focused, supported by an inclusive and fun company culture. Our clients value our proactive approach, depth of expertise, integrity and the quality we deliver. As a result, our people get to enjoy working on some of the most exciting projects in the world. **Job Description** Due to growing demand for our consultancy services, we are seeking a consultant responsible for the configuration administration, and ongoing optimisation of project management systems and associated reporting tools in Power BI. The role ensures the stability, accuracy, and usability of the project management environment, supports users across the organisation and maintains dashboards that provide real‑time performance insight for projects, programmes, and portfolios. **Key Responsibilities** * Configure, maintain and optimise project management systems and related features * Manage workflows, custom fields, lookup tables and security permissions * Monitor system performance, perform routine housekeeping and ensure high platform availability * Coordinate with client’s IT team on patching, updates and system‑related issues * Maintain and enhance dashboards in Power BI (or equivalent BI tools) * Ensure data refresh schedules, connections and gateway configurations are reliable and secure * Provide day‑to‑day user support, troubleshooting and issue resolution for users * Develop training materials, user guides, and process documentation * Conduct onboarding and refresher training sessions for project managers, schedulers and PMO teams * Identify opportunities to enhance system functionality, user experience, and data quality **Qualifications** * Minimum of 1 year of experience in administering and maintaining systems and dashboards * Bachelor’s degree in STEM related subjects, e.g. Mathematics, Computer Science * Good knowledge of construction industry technical matters, such as procurement, design, construction and handover * Strong understanding of project, programme, and portfolio management processes * Hands\-on experience in administering project management systems (e.g. MS Project Web App, Oracle Primavera Cloud, etc.) * Hands\-on experience in reporting tools such as Power BI Desirable Skills and Accreditations * Knowledge of PMO frameworks (PRINCE2, APM, PMI) * Familiarity with SQL, DAX, or data modelling **Additional Information** Our people share our Purpose and Values. Turner \& Townsend provides a great place to work, where everyone can make change happen and influence a better world.​ We champion our people to succeed in both work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive, and flexible working environment that respects work\-life balance.​ Turner \& Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.​ If this sounds like a challenge you’d like to hear more about, please submit an application for our recruitment team to review. ​ Please find out more about us at www.turnerandtownsend.com/​ Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner \& Townsend and our exciting future projects: Twitter Instagram LinkedIn It is strictly against Turner \& Townsend policy for candidates to pay any fee in relation to our recruitment process. No recruitment agency working with Turner \& Townsend will ask candidates to pay a fee at any time. Any unsolicited resumes/CVs submitted through our website or to Turner \& Townsend personal e\-mail accounts, are considered property of Turner \& Townsend and are not subject to payment of agency fees. In order to be an authorised Recruitment Agency/Search Firm for Turner \& Townsend, there must be a formal written agreement in place and the agency must be invited, by the Recruitment Team, to submit candidates for review. Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner \& Townsend and our exciting future projects: Twitter Instagram LinkedIn *It is strictly against Turner \& Townsend policy for candidates to pay any fee in relation to our recruitment process. No recruitment agency working with Turner \& Townsend will ask candidates to pay a fee at any time.* *Any unsolicited resumes/CVs submitted through our website or to Turner \& Townsend personal e\-mail accounts, are considered property of Turner \& Townsend and are not subject to payment of agency fees. In order to be an authorised Recruitment Agency/Search Firm for Turner \& Townsend, there must be a formal written agreement in place and the agency must be invited, by the Recruitment Team, to submit candidates for review.*

title: Customer Experience Champion - Mullingar
description: PTSB is one of Ireland's leading retail and SME banks, with an innovative range of products and services powered through an evolving digital landscape, our focus is centred on ensuring we deliver what our customers, colleagues and communities need to be successful. As a Branch Customer Experience Champion, you will work collaboratively with your team and Branch Lead to support the efficient delivery of business objectives and the day to day branch operations in a compliant manner. You will actively develop your skills through participation in our Collaborative Customer Conversations program whilst ensuring you meet the needs of our customers through promotion of our Omni\-Channel approach. You will become familiar with the Banks Customer Segmentation Strategy and take part in \`in branch' and localised promotional activities within the territory to identify new business opportunities to increase new customer acquisition whilst maintaining positive relationships with existing customers. **Responsibilities:** * Support in the day to day operation of customer service within the branch. * Provide an excellent level of customer service and advise our customers throughout their product /financial needs journey, further improving the customer experience with both over the phone and face to face interactions. * Take ownership and deal with customer queries in an effective, professional and compliant manner. * Generate and execute sales from lead (where qualified to do so) to fulfilment in accordance with the Omni\-Channel ethos and activity management system. * Assist with sales campaigns including post sales fulfilment, administration and follow up tasks. * Adopt a prompt and customer centred response to leads passed from Open24 to maximise new business opportunities from customer base. **Requirements:** * QFA or APA in Loans and/or Savings \& Investments, with up\-to\-date CPD hours for the relevant CPD years. If no APA held, or only one APA in Loans or S\&I held, the candidate must commit to qualifying within a 2 year period to achieve APA in both Loans and S\&I. * If no APA held, the candidate must meet the minimum entry requirements of holding an Ordinary Leaving Certificate (or equivalent) with a grade D3/O6/H6 at Ordinary or Higher Level in five Leaving Certificate subjects (including English and Maths), and/or 5\+ years post Leaving Certificate experience. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills with a commitment to providing an outstanding customer experience * Have significant level of proven sales, customer service or clerical experience in a regulatory compliant environment (where MCC) * Committed to and enjoys working in a sales environment **This is a Permanent role, based in Mullingar (Onsite).** Is this you? Please apply online on our website or via the apply link of this role. Your application will be sent through to our Talent Acquisition team and they will be in touch regarding your application. We reserve the right to draw up a shortlist for interview. The Bank understands the importance of a consistent and relentless focus on championing diversity and inclusion. We aim to attract, recruit, and retain individuals with diverse backgrounds, skills, competencies and abilities to work collaboratively to enhance the service we provide to all of our customers and the communities we serve.

title: Cyber Security Analyst
description: My Dublin based client is recruiting for a Cyber Security Risk Analyst to join a growing team on a permanent basis. This role requires 1 day per week onsite. The Cyber Security Risk Analyst will be responsible for establishing and maintaining a strong security posture across the organisation. The team defines security strategies, frameworks, and controls, and ensures their effective implementation to protect information assets and manage security risks. **Responsibilities** * Act as a primary point of contact for information security matters related to service delivery and operational activities. * Promote awareness and understanding of information security principles, policies, and processes across the organisation. * Identify, assess, and monitor information security risks in collaboration with risk owners, ensuring appropriate controls are applied throughout the service and system lifecycle. * Evaluate the effectiveness of information security controls and ensure alignment with organisational policies, regulatory obligations, and recognised industry standards. * Develop, maintain, and improve information security policies, standards, and procedures. * Support third\-party risk management activities, including defining and assessing security requirements for suppliers and outsourced services. * Lead or contribute to security initiatives and improvement projects, working with cross\-functional teams to enhance security processes and increase automation where appropriate. **Skills / Experience Required** * A degree in computer science, information technology, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience. * 5\+ years of experience in information security or related IT roles, with strong exposure to risk management, security frameworks, and control design. * Broad knowledge of information security technologies, architectures, and operational processes, including information security management systems and industry standards (e.g. ISO 27001, COBIT, DORA, NIS2\). * Experience working with data, reporting, automation, or AI\-enabled tools to support security processes. * Experience managing or assessing security risks associated with third\-party suppliers, cloud services, and outsourced environments. * Proven ability to manage projects and influence stakeholders in complex organisational environments. * Strong communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, professional documentation and communicate effectively with technical and non\-technical audiences. **Benefits** Hybrid Working \- Only 1 Day per week required on site Healthcare Bonus Childcare Allowance Pension Sub Canteen

title: Data Analyst
description: **About The Role** We are seeking a highly analytical and detail\-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for transforming complex data into understandable and conclusive insights. You will play a key role in training different business areas on the effective use of our Business Intelligence (BI) tools and advising them on how to maximize the value derived from our data. If you have a passion for data storytelling, strong communication skills, and enjoy empowering others with data, we encourage you to apply. **Responsibilities** * Collect, clean, analyze, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights. * Create clear, concise, and impactful visualizations, reports, and dashboards that effectively communicate findings to various stakeholders. * Develop and deliver training sessions to educate different business units on the functionality and best practices of BI tools. * Provide expert advice and guidance to the business area on how to leverage data to answer key business questions and drive informed decision\-making. * Collaborate with data engineers and other teams to ensure data accuracy, accessibility, and consistency. * Identify opportunities for process improvements and data optimization. * Stay up\-to\-date with industry best practices and emerging trends in data analysis, BI tools, and data visualization. **Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Business Analytics, or a Business related discipline. * Proven experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role. * Strong proficiency in SQL. * Extensive experience with Business Intelligence (BI) tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker, Qlik Sense). * Excellent data visualization skills and the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and critical thinking abilities. * Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non\-technical audiences. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced environment. * Detail\-oriented with a strong commitment to data accuracy and integrity.

title: Data Analyst
description: **ROLE:** Data Analyst **LOCATION: Dublin, Perm, Hybrid** ***ABOUT THE ROLE:*** Barden are delighted to be partnering with our Client on a new opportunity for an **experienced Data Analyst** . This role sits within their Group Commercial Team and will someone with circa 4\-5 years’ experience across data storytelling, dashboard development and commercial performance. This role will support strategic decision making by delivering actionable insights through Power BI dashboard reporting, working closely with internal stakeholders across the Group. **Key Responsibilities:** The core responsibilities will centre around the following areas, and will evolve depending on the needs of the business: * Develop, maintain, and optimise interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI to support commercial decision\-making. * Deliver commercial insights and reporting at a group level, ensuring alignment across all regions. * Collaborate with internal stakeholders to gather requirements, understand business needs, and translate them into actionable data solutions. * Assist in the development of automated reporting processes. * Contribute to ad\-hoc analysis and deep dives to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities. * Support the cross\-functional taskforce focused on aligning KPIs across regions, contributing to consistency and comparability of commercial performance metrics. * Ensure data governance and compliance with internal standards. ***ABOUT THE PERSON:*** **Technical Skills:** * Strong proficiency in Power BI and its components (e.g. DAX, Power Query) essential. * Experience working with large datasets and data modelling. * Knowledge of SQL, or Python is a plus. * Ability to translate business requirements into effective data visualisations. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with excellent attention to detail. * Commercially minded individual, with a strong understanding of how data and insights drive business performance and decision\-making. * Excellent communication skills and ability to present insights to non\-technical audience.

title: Data Engineer III
description: Overview As a Data Engineer III at Fanatics Betting \& Gaming (FBG), you will play a crucial role in the development, deployment, and management of our data architecture. In this role you will be responsible for building systems and user experiences for our internal customers that are able to aggregate customer data across many sources, integrate with various 3rd party systems and build personalized experiences that allow us to deliver a great and rewarding experience to our customers. You will collaborate with a team of experts to design and implement systems that collect, manage, and convert raw data into usable information for data analysts and business users to interpret. This position is perfect for individuals who are passionate about data, have a knack for problem\-solving, and possess a strong foundation in data manipulation and statistical analysis. We are one team; we employ the principles of data\-driven development, operational excellence, process calibration, and efficiency to deliver a stable, extensible platform at scale. Responsibilities \- Assist in the design and construction of scalable data management systems, ensuring they meet business requirements and industry practices \- Design, build, and maintain scalable and robust data pipelines to support analytics, business intelligence, and machine learning models \- Implement and maintain databases and data processing systems to support data collection, integration, and retention requirements according to company policies \- Develop and optimize data pipelines, ensuring efficient and error\-free data flow across the organization \- Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to understand data needs and implement solutions that enable efficient data analysis and reporting. \- Monitor the performance of data processing and storage systems, resolving any failures and ensuring data accuracy and security \- Implement data governance and security best practices to ensure data integrity and compliance with relevant regulations. \- Develop a fundamental understanding of our industry, product, feature set and roadmap \- Provide technical leadership and mentorship to junior data engineers, promoting a culture of excellence and continuous learning \- Help to onboard new team members \- Stay updated with emerging trends and technologies in data engineering, applying new techniques and tools to improve our data ecosystem \- Must be open to occasional travel to Fanatics Betting \& Gaming offices and other locations for events, meetings, and team\-building activities Required Qualifications \- 5\+ years of data engineering experience with a proven track record of helping to design and implement large\-scale data solutions \- Possess a strong foundation in data structures, algorithms, and software engineering principles \- Demonstrated skills writing SQL queries \- Familiar with other data languages including R or Python, and tools like Tableau, Microstrategy, Redshift, or Snowflake \- Knowledgeable about data modeling, ETL development, and data warehousing principles \- Familiar with SQL and NoSQL databases, including MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, or Cassandra \- Solid analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy \- Excellent communication and collaboration skills \- Intellectually curious and interested in solving difficult problems \- Able to effectively communicate complex technical subjects to a variety of audiences (engineers, technical leaders, marketing and operations teams, non\-technical functional leaders) \- You have an agile mindset; able to embrace new initiatives in a fast\-paced environment \- Thrives in an entrepreneurial, hyper\-growth environment where priorities evolve regularly and decisions are made quickly \- Team\-first mentality, with a willingness to do what it takes to get the job done and a desire to strive for better Preferred Qualifications \- Experience working in a start\-up environment If you possess some of these qualifications but not all of them, we still encourage you to apply!

title: Design Project Engineer - Mechanical, Fleet Remediation Engineering
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- As an Amazon Project Design Mechanical Engineer, you will lead design projects in operational data centers, that remediate issues, upgrade our existing infrastructure or add new services. You will be the 'go to' engineering resource for these projects and will provide lifecycle support from design through implementation. You will have the opportunity to work on projects locally and globally. You will use your mechanical expertise to: * Own the design for existing data center upgrades and design\-solutions which add data center infrastructure, capacity, improve availability, and/or increase sustainability. * Perform design and equipment submittal review for Data Centers in your region. * Interface with internal and external data center design engineering team, server hardware team,environmental health and safety team, field engineering, and controls engineering to promote standards that maintain consistency and reliability in services delivered. * Work on concurrent projects, sometimes in multiple geographical regions. * Act as technical subject matter expert for your given discipline during new remediation projects. * Support construction, commissioning, operations, and turnover. Amazon's vision is to be the world's most customer\-centric company, and this role is key to that vision. As a Fleet Remediation Project Design Mechanical Engineer, you will be leading projects to fit out our data centers to meet ever\-evolving customer needs as we continue expanding our fleet to hyper\-scale. About AIS AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. Key job responsibilities * Perform design and equipment submittal review for Data Centers in your region. * Interface with internal and external data center design engineering team, server hardware team, environmental health and safety team, field engineering, and controls engineering to promote standards that maintain consistency and reliability in services delivered. * Work on concurrent projects, sometimes in multiple geographical regions. * Act as a technical subject matter expert for your given discipline during new remediation projects. * Support construction, commissioning, operations, and turnover. * Possess strong engineering judgement and are able to provide design and recommendations despite uncertainty * Are detail and data oriented with familiarity with design/construction processes * Have experience managing engineering projects and engineering consultants. * Build trust and relationships with different stakeholders (e.g., Operations, Commissioning, Construction and Design) * Manage design and as built drawings and documents * Prepare and present progress reports to all relevant stakeholders * Be inclined to get into the field to see things up close A day in the life Each day you will interact with different teams responsible for various aspects of the data centers. You will mentor and oversee the work of junior engineers in your discipline. You will have the opportunity to work on projects locally and globally, with up to 25% travel. About the team Our Design Engineers drive data center infrastructure projects with strong engineering judgment, attention to detail, and hands\-on field experience. They manage projects and consultants, build cross\-functional relationships, oversee design documentation, and prioritize initiatives that maximize impact on safety, capacity, and availability. Why AWS? Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Knowledge of mechanical or electrical systems design * Experience reading, interpreting, and creating construction drawings, specifications, and submittal documents * Experience in industrial or commercial engineering in mission critical facilities including but not limited to: data centers, power generation or oil and gas facilities * Bachelor's degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or a related field **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Professional Engineer License * Experience with building codes and regulations for your region * Experience carrying design concepts through exploration, development, and into deployment or mass production Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: DevOps BizOps Engineer
description: Job Description* Job Title: DevOps BizOps Engineer City: Dublin State/Province: Dublin Posting Start Date: 4/7/26 Wipro Limited (NYSE: WIT, BSE: 507685, NSE: WIPRO) is a leading technology services and consulting company focused on building innovative solutions that address clients’ most complex digital transformation needs. Leveraging our holistic portfolio of capabilities in consulting, design, engineering, and operations, we help clients realize their boldest ambitions and build future\-ready, sustainable businesses. With over 230,000 employees and business partners across 65 countries, we deliver on the promise of helping our customers, colleagues, and communities thrive in an ever\-changing world. For additional information, visit us at www.wipro.com. Job Description: **Job Description** ------------------- As a BizOps Senior Engineer, you will act as the production readiness steward for versatile Gateway products and integration with other platforms. You will partner with development teams to design, implement, and support services with a focus on operational resilience, automation, and compliance. Should have knowledge of Payment domains specially in Gateways with least gRPC experience and strong with SRE fundamentals and hands\-on experience **Key Responsibilities** * Lifecycle Ownership: Engage in and improve the entire service lifecycle—from design and deployment to operations and continuous improvement. * Operational Readiness: Ensure system availability, capacity, performance, monitoring, and self\-healing capabilities are embedded throughout delivery. * Incident Management: Practice sustainable incident response, lead blameless postmortems, and optimize Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR). * Develop and maintain automation pipelines for certificate renewal, traffic routing, alerting, and compliance reporting using tools like Ansible, Venafi \& XLR template. * Support CI/CD pipelines for software promotion and operational gating. * Reliability Engineering: Scale systems sustainably through automation and advocate for changes that improve reliability and velocity. * Compliance \& Risk Management: Drive initiatives for Safety \& Soundness, PCI compliance, threat/toil reduction, and ITSM defect resolution. * Monitoring \& Observability: Implement robust logging, monitoring, and alerting standards to ensure system health and proactive issue detection. Hands\-on experience with Dynatrace \& Splunk monitoring tool configuration and alerting. * Collaboration: Work with global teams across multiple time zones and mentor junior engineers. Continuous Improvement: Provide feedback loops to development teams on resiliency gaps and operational enhancements. * **Required Experience \& Skills** * Experience: Extensive experience in BizOps, Site Reliability Engineering, or DevOps roles. * Strong understanding of NGINX configuration and gRPC event\-driven architectures. * Proficiency in DevOps tools: Chef, Jenkins, Groovy, shell scripting, Bitbucket, Git, Ansible, XLR. * Experience with AWS infrastructure, secure access practices, and cloud\-native deployments. * Security \& Compliance: * Awareness of certificate lifecycle management, mutual TLS, SSL handshake, SSH keys, encryption standards. * Familiarity with ITSM processes, compliance frameworks, and incident management. * Networking \& Systems: * Knowledge of client\-server relationships, network layers (L1–L7\), load balancers (BIG\-IP F5\), and application firewalls. * Ability to analyze stack traces, TCP dumps, heap/thread dumps, and perform OS\-level troubleshooting. * Authentication \& Authorization: * Intermediate understanding of Active Directory, SAML, LTPA, SSO, OAuth. **͏** ----- **Deliver** **No** **Performance Parameter** **Measure** 1\. Continuous Integration,Deployment \& Monitoring 100% error free on boarding \& implementation 2\. CSAT Timely customer resolution as per TAT Zero escalation **͏** ----- **͏** ----- Mandatory Skills: Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) . Experience: 3\-5 Years . Reinvent your world. We are building a modern Wipro. We are an end\-to\-end digital transformation partner with the boldest ambitions. To realize them, we need people inspired by reinvention. Of yourself, your career, and your skills. We want to see the constant evolution of our business and our industry. It has always been in our DNA \- as the world around us changes, so do we. Join a business powered by purpose and a place that empowers you to design your own reinvention.

title: ELECTRICAL ENGINEER (2 POSITIONS AVAILABLE)
description: Reporting to the Project Manager/ Engineering Manager **Duties** * Liaising with clients and assisting in managing Electrical projects * Site surveys and Engineering inspection activities * Analysis and review of Electrical design documents * Electrical Specification writing, trouble shooting and problem solving * Provide Technical support to the Electrical Site Management Team * Manage the Electrical documentation to ensure the relevant Registers and Logs are kept up to date * Manage Electrical System handovers, with site\-based Commissioning specialists * Evaluate RFI responses and ensure engineering issues are resolved * Ensure CJK Engineering Site Management are working off the latest Electrical Specifications and Drawings * Prepare Electrical Technical Submittals according to the specifications * Populate Electrical Test Packs and collated Electrical Test record sheets for system completion. * Collate Electrical O\&M Manual documentation for inclusion in the final handover documents * Ensure the Project Quality Plan and associated Electrical Inspection \& Test Plan is being followed for each Electrical system * Manage QA/QC processes * Support commissioning activities including walk\-downs, testing and handover preparations * Maintain red\-lines and review as\-builts throughout project lifecycle and handover * Mentor graduate Engineers * Collaborating with site teams, vendors, clients and consultations * Developing System Installation Work Package **Previous Experience / Qualifications** * Minimum 3\-5 years experience * Third Level qualification or currently studying Electrical Engineering or Building Services Engineering * Ability to work well within strict deadlines and on their own initiative * Good communication skills * Strong Microsoft Excel skills * Excellent attention to detail * Electrical knowledge \& experience gained from either a trade or academic background **Desirable** * Good knowledge of AutoCad / NavisWorks * Previous construction site experience * Renewable energy knowledge beneficial

title: Eirdata Graduate Engineer
description: **Who we are;** **Established in 2001, Eirdata are specialists in cleanroom validation, commissioning \& compliance. We provide these range of services to the pharmaceutical, medical devices and healthcare sectors primarily. In 2013, Eirdata was acquired by ESS Group.** **ESS Group includes three companies – Engineering Support \& Services Ltd. (ESS), Eirdata Environmental Services Ltd. (Eirdata), and the newly joined Critical Airflow Europe (CAE).*** **ESS are experts in asset management and maintenance reliability through people.** * **Eirdata are specialists in cleanroom validation, commissioning and compliance, HVAC systems, indoor air quality and building wellness.** * **CAE are the European leader in delivering safe energy efficient laboratory control and critical airflow applications. Critical Airflow Europe Ltd represents Phoenix, Strobic and Aircuity.** **Summary of the Role** The aim of the Graduate Programme is to develop the best and brightest engineering talent to become the future leaders. Within Eirdata you will be part of a multi\-skilled and adaptable team, ready and prepared to take on a wide variety of technical challenges throughout various industries. All our structured graduate programmes provide a collaborative, open environment where graduates have a voice to bring their own great ideas in the business. The programme also supports graduates in developing real\-world skills such as project management, communication, and technical role\-specific skills, among others. **Our Goal** * Establish a flexible and multi\-skilled workforce capable of evolving the business landscape. * Develop, motivate, and retain our graduates. * Maximise the knowledge and experience within our company and ensure that this knowledge is captured and updated effectively. * Provide skills for future innovations within Eirdata. **Duties \& Main Responsibilities*** Commissioning, troubleshooting, testing and validation of HVAC systems including GxP HVAC Systems, LEV’s and Fume Cupboards and associated utilities systems. * HEPA Filter Integrity Testing * Particle Count testing * Verification of cleanroom air change rates and Cleanroom pressure profiles * HVAC Air and Water Systems Balancing and Commissioning * HVAC Systems Trouble Shooting and Fault Finding * Complete validation documentation, check out sheets and complete detailed HVAC commissioning reports. * Technical support * Good Documentation Practices (Knowledge of ALCOA\+ principles) **Other Duties as part of the team** * Energy \- Survey and implementation of energy saving projects. * External audit preparation and support. * New installations, modifications, and improvements to existing systems. **Education \& Qualifications*** Relevant Engineering Degree L7/8 \- Mechanical/Electrical/Building Services * Excellent communication skills both written and verbal. * Computer Literate. * Ability to work on own initiative. **Required Skills** * Working knowledge of Microsoft office (excel, word, PowerPoint). * Team player, with a flexible approach but also able to work under own initiative. * Strong verbal and interpersonal skills * Competent level of technical writing and understanding of technical documentation. * Full clean driving licence required. * Must be eligible to work in Ireland. **Benefits** Eirdata offer support that enables you to never stop growing. Your reward at Eirdata is not just your salary, we believe in investing in our people's personal, professional development and empowering their work\-life balance to enable continuous growth. We offer supports and benefits that suit you, wherever you are in life, including Health care, pension, training, coaching. * Competitive Salay * Very attractive pension scheme * VHI * Laptop * Phone * Paid Annual Leave * Supporting your work life balance

title: Electrical Estimator
description: **TDE Group – Electrical Contractors** Kilcullen, Co. Kildare TDE Group are an expanding electrical contractor delivering commercial, industrial and fit\-out projects across Ireland. Our team has grown rapidly in recent years and we are now seeking an Electrical Estimator / Junior Estimator to support the preparation of electrical tenders and pricing of upcoming projects. Our recent work includes warehouse and logistics projects, commercial fit\-outs across Ireland. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with an electrical trade background or construction estimating experience to develop their career within a growing company. Key Responsibilities * Review tender drawings, specifications and project documentation to understand project scope and requirements. * Carry out detailed quantity take\-offs from electrical drawings including lighting, power, containment, distribution, fire alarm and specialist systems. * Assist in preparing detailed cost estimates including labour, materials, plant and subcontract packages. * Issue supplier and subcontractor enquiries, ensuring competitive quotations are obtained within tender deadlines. * Analyse supplier quotations and ensure all items are compliant with the project specification and scope. * Prepare Bills of Quantities and pricing schedules for tender submissions. * Assist in compiling complete tender submissions, including pricing breakdowns, clarifications and supporting documentation. * Maintain and develop an internal cost database including labour rates, material pricing and historical project costs. * Liaise with suppliers, subcontractors and manufacturers to obtain technical and commercial information required for tenders. * Attend site visits and tender walkdowns where required to assess installation conditions and logistics. * Work with the directors to review tender risk, programme durations and commercial considerations. * Support post\-tender clarifications and negotiations with clients or main contractors. * Assist with project handover to the delivery team once contracts are awarded. * Maintain accurate records of tender submissions, pricing information and supplier quotations Requirements * Electrical trade background or construction related qualification preferred * Ability to read and interpret electrical drawings * Strong organisational and communication skills * Good knowledge of Microsoft Excel and Office * Attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines * Full driving licence What We Offer * Salary €35,000 – €45,000 depending on experience * Opportunity to progress into a senior estimating role * Exposure to commercial and industrial electrical projects * A supportive working environment within a growing contractor * Location: Kilcullen, Co. Kildare * Apply by sending CV to: Info@TDEGroup.ie Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €35,000\.00\-€45,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Employment Associate
description: **Employment Solicitor (1\-4 PQE) \| Top Tier Law Firm \| Dublin** We are currently working with a top tier Dublin law firm seeking to hire a talented Employment Solicitor (0\-4 PQE) to join its highly regarded and growing team. This is an excellent opportunity for a junior to mid\-level solicitor to develop their career within a market\-leading practice advising a high\-profile domestic and international client base. The Role This team advises on a broad range of employment law matters, both contentious and non\-contentious, including: * Employment contracts, policies, and HR advisory work * Workplace investigations and disciplinary processes * Employment aspects of corporate transactions (M\&A support) * Litigation before the WRC, Labour Court, and civil courts * Redundancy and restructuring projects * Equality and regulatory matters You will work closely with experienced partners on complex, high\-value matters and gain exposure to top\-tier clients across a variety of sectors. The Candidate The ideal candidate will have: * 1\-4 years' post\-qualification experience in employment law * Experience from a leading firm * Excellent technical ability and attention to detail * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * A genuine interest in developing a long\-term career in employment law **\*\*\* Be admitted as a Solicitor in Ireland** The Opportunity This firm offers: * Exposure to some of the most high\-profile employment work in the Irish market * A supportive and collaborative team environment * Excellent training and career progression opportunities * Competitive remuneration and benefits package * Hybrid working model This is a fantastic opportunity to join a top tier firm at an exciting stage of growth. **For more information or a confidential discussion, please get in touch briona.brogan@robertwalters.com.** **Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates**

title: Employment Lawyer - Snr Associate
description: **Employment Law Senior Associate** Our Client, a **mid tier law firm in Dublin 2** currently has a vacancy for an **Employment Law Solicitor with 4\-6 years’ PQE** . This is a great opportunity for a solicitor who wants to provide high\-calibre advice, build strong client relationships, and contribute to the continued growth of a busy Employment Law practice. **Talk to Cormac Spencer on 01 845 4651 for more information.** **Salary:** Up to €125k **Benefits:** Bonus, Pension, Hybrid Working (2 days WFH), Healthcare, **Total Package: up to €160k** **Required Experience:** * Experience in assisting employers with the full range of legal issues that may arise during the employment relationship, from recruitment to exit; * Advising on risk management and employment issues during corporate transactions and transfer of undertakings; * International labour and employment law experience is highly valued * Strong experience handling volumes of WRC and Labour Court cases * Experience of advising employers on day\-to\-day HR issues such as managing disciplinary processes, employment contracts, restrictive covenants, pension issues and statutory entitlements; and * Understanding of public sector specific employment law. **Key Competencies:** * Excellent drafting skills; * An ability to generate new client relationships and maintain and expand existing client relationships; * Collaborating at all levels with an ability to communicate points clearly; * A calm and measured approach to problem solving; * A willingness to attend networking and marketing events, when required. **If this Employment Law Solicitor role in Dublin sounds like your next move, call Cormac NOW on 01 845 4651 for full details.** **Speak to the Experts with the Direct Link to the Best Solicitor Jobs in Ireland.** **Link Personnel** www.linkpersonnel.ie

title: Executive Associate (Strategy, Data & Delivery)
description: The Executive Associate, DCEO Office (Operations \& Insights) is a high‑impact, early‑career role that blends executive support, data analysis, project delivery, and board‑level storytelling. Partnering directly with the DCEO, you will orchestrate the rhythm of the business, secure timely, high‑quality inputs from functions and service providers, and transform complex data into crisp narratives and decision materials. The role flexes with the CEO’s priorities week‑to‑week, ensuring momentum, clarity, and results. **Key Responsibilities** * Run the DCEO Rhythm of the Business: own the annual, quarterly, monthly, and weekly cadence, including inputs, deadlines, and deliverables. * Board \& ExComm reporting: shape storylines, synthesise metrics, and produce clear, visually compelling packs that “bring the requisite audience on a journey” without overloading figures. * Insight generation: translate operational and financial data into trends, risks, opportunities, and recommended actions. * Data pipeline coordination: secure timely, complete, and high‑quality data from functions and external service providers; resolve gaps and inconsistencies fast. * Presentation excellence: build executive‑level slides and visuals in PowerPoint, uphold a consistent design system and narrative structure. * Project management: plan, track, and deliver DCEO‑sponsored initiatives, manage scope and comms. * Stakeholder orchestration: align C\-Suite colleagues, Directors, Finance, HR, Medical, Clinical/Ops, IT, and external partners around DCEO priorities. * Decision support: prepare succinct decision memos (“one‑pagers”), options, trade‑offs, and clear recommendations. * Executive briefings: package pre‑reads and talking points for internal and external meetings; ensure pre‑wire with key stakeholders. * Follow‑through: capture meeting actions, their owners, and due dates, then drive to closure and surface slippage early. * Issue escalation: spot signals in sow downs or failures, escalate the right issues to the DCEO with context and proposed fixes. * Confidentiality \& governance: handle sensitive information with discretion, follow information‑security and approvals protocols. * Calendar and priority triage: protect DCEO focus time, sequence work to match shifting priorities. * Vendor/service‑provider management: set expectations, SLAs, and delivery quality for data and content inputs. * KPI / dashboard upkeep: maintain a concise “single source of truth” for top metrics and update cadence. * Change agility: rapidly re‑plan when priorities move, communicate impacts and next steps. * Process improvement: simplify reporting cycles and automate where practical (templates, checklists, light scripts). * Internal communications: draft professional crisp updates from DCEO office as required. * Knowledge management: maintain a searchable, version‑controlled repository of past packs, decisions, and templates. * Culture \& standards: model pace, professionalism, and high bar for quality across all deliverables. **Person Requirements** * Early‑career track record (typically 2–4 years) in one or more of: analytics, strategy/ops, PMO, management consulting, Human Resources or high‑performing EA/Chief‑of‑Staff‑associate roles. * Outstanding PowerPoint: visual storytelling, executive‑level slide craft, and narrative design. * Strong quantitative literacy: Excel/Sheets for analysis; comfort with BI outputs (Power BI/Tableau) and manipulating datasets. * Project delivery: plans, stakeholder management, and a reputation for closing actions. * Executive presence \& writing: concise briefs, decision memos, and clear meeting pre‑reads. * Stakeholder savvy: confident with senior leaders, can secure inputs without formal authority. * Bias for action: self‑starter, organised, and calm under shifting priorities and short notice. * High integrity \& discretion handling confidential information. **Desirable Requirements** * Exposure to board reporting or investor‑style narratives. * Power BI/Tableau basics; familiarity with SharePoint/OneDrive for structured content management. * PMP/PRINCE2, Agile foundations, or analytics/data‑viz coursework/certifications. * Experience working with external vendors/service providers and SLAs. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree (business, economics, analytics, engineering, or related) or equivalent practical experience. THE MATER PRIVATE IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES EMPLOYER

title: FP&A Analyst
description: **We are partnering with a major national energy organisation to appoint an FP\&A Analyst. This is a key business‑facing role that blends financial modelling, forecasting, performance analysis, and cross‑functional stakeholder engagement.** **The position offers strong exposure to senior management, ownership of budgeting and planning cycles, and the opportunity to influence decision‑making through high‑quality financial insight and commentary.** **Responsibilities:** * Leading the budgeting, forecasting, and long‑term planning processes, ensuring timely and accurate submissions. * Partnering with business units to understand financial drivers, identify risks and opportunities, and support commercial decision‑making. * Analysing monthly performance, preparing clear variance analysis, insights, and presentations for senior stakeholders. * Developing and enhancing financial models, dashboards, and analytical tools to improve visibility and accuracy. * Driving continuous improvement and supporting automation across FP\&A processes. * Ensuring strong governance, control compliance, and alignment with audit standards. **Requirements:** * Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) with at least 2 years’ post‑qualification experience. * Excellent financial, analytical, and problem‑solving skills. * Strong Excel capability with experience in data‑driven analysis; Power BI or similar tools an advantage. * Confident communicator with the ability to engage and challenge senior stakeholders effectively. * Highly organised, detail‑oriented, and comfortable managing competing priorities. * Experience with budgeting, forecasting, or financial modelling beneficial; familiarity with SAP or other planning systems a plus. **Our client offers a hybrid working environment (minimum two days on‑site), flexible working arrangements, and the opportunity to contribute to Ireland’s transition to a net‑zero energy system.** Apply directly via the link provided or contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: Finance Assistant
description: **Finance Assistant – Fixed\-Term Contract (Until June 2027\)** **North Dublin (10 minutes from Ashbourne)\| Must Drive \& Have Vehicle (Important)** **Full\-Time Onsite\| 9am–5:30pm** **Free Parking** A leading organisation in the **logistics sector** is seeking a proactive and detail‑driven **Finance Assistant** to join their team on a long\-term fixed\-term contract until June 2027\. This is a fantastic opportunity for someone who enjoys variety, ownership, and working in a fast\-paced environment where no two days look the same. The role is based in **North Dublin** , just a short 10‑minute drive from Ashbourne, making it ideal for candidates with their own transport. You’ll be joining a collaborative finance team and supporting a wide range of accounting and reporting activities across the business. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Managing the processing of purchase orders and invoices, ensuring accuracy and timely entry. * Investigating and resolving overhead‑related queries, identifying pricing discrepancies and cost‑saving opportunities. * Reviewing vendor accounts, completing reconciliations, and setting up new suppliers. * Preparing statutory submissions such as VAT and other required returns. * Supporting month‑end close activities, including accruals, prepayments, overhead reporting, and product‑level margin reviews. * Assisting with revenue and cost reconciliations across multiple systems. * Providing general finance support across the team as needed. **What We’re Looking For** * Previous experience in an accounting or finance support role. * Strong Excel skills and confidence working with Microsoft Office tools. * Someone who is **curious, quick to learn, and not afraid to ask questions** . * Highly organised with the ability to prioritise workload and meet deadlines. * Strong communication skills and a collaborative approach. * Self‑motivated, adaptable, and comfortable working in a busy environment. * Must have a **full driving license and access to a car.** **Why Join?** * Long\-term Fixed Term role (12 \- 13 months) with stability through to mid 2027\. * Supportive team environment with regular company events. * Opportunity to broaden your finance experience across multiple areas. * Free onsite parking

title: Finance Associate
description: **We are supporting a leading global investment firm to appoint a Finance Associate / Senior Finance Associate.** **This role is ideal for Big‑4 professionals seeking their first move into industry, or someone with 1\-2 years' PQE offering exposure to complex investment structures, financial reporting, and cross‑functional collaboration within a sophisticated alternatives platform.** **Responsibilities:** * Support senior finance leadership across a range of fund‑level reporting and operational finance activities. * Review investment structures and assess accounting, tax, and regulatory considerations. * Assist with investor reporting, fund performance analysis, and ad‑hoc information requests. * Contribute to the preparation and review of quarterly and annual financial statements. * Coordinate audit processes and liaise with external advisors. * Support reviews of valuations, capital activity, and fee calculations. **Requirements:** * Newly Qualified Accountant from Big‑4 audit. (FS audit, asset management, banking, or structured finance advantageous). Or 1\-2 years' PQE in funds. * Strong technical grounding in accounting, financial reporting, and controls. * Excellent analytical skills and strong attention to detail. * Comfortable interpreting complex financial documentation. * Confident communicator with the ability to work collaboratively across teams. * Advanced Excel skills; professional qualification (ACA/ACCA/CPA) required or nearing completion. **This is a great opportunity to step directly into the finance team of a global investment organisation, gaining exposure to alternative investment strategies, sophisticated fund structures, and a high‑performance international environment. The role is Dublin‑based with hybrid working and strong long‑term career progression.** Apply in confidence or contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: Financial Analyst
description: **We are seeking a Financial Analyst to join a growing organisation operating within the energy and infrastructure sector, to support a portfolio of large\-scale projects. The business is operating in a capital\-intensive environment and continues to expand, offering strong exposure across project delivery, commercial decision\-making, and senior stakeholder engagement.** **Key Responsibilities:** * Partner with project and senior stakeholders to provide financial insight, challenge assumptions, and support decision\-making. * Support budgeting, forecasting, and business case development, including scenario analysis and cost evaluation. * Prepare and review monthly management accounts, including variance analysis and clear performance commentary. * Maintain strong financial controls and support audit, compliance, and governance requirements across projects. * Contribute to financial planning, cashflow forecasting, and the ongoing development of finance processes. **Key Requirements:** * Qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA / CIMA or equivalent) with approximately 1–3 years’ post\-qualification experience. * Experience in a finance business partnering or commercially focused finance role. * Background in a capital\-intensive environment such as energy, infrastructure, construction, or property. * Strong experience in management accounting, budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis. * Confident communicator with the ability to engage and challenge non\-finance stakeholders. **This role offers the opportunity to develop business partnering experience within a commercial finance environment, supporting key projects within a Joint Venture alongside major industry organisations.** For a confidential discussion, please contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: Food & Beverage Assistant
description: NYX Hotels is giving laidback luxury: we are THE destination! Our NYX team (we call them City Lovers!) is a diverse bunch of vibrant people, who are on a mission to make NYX the first\-choice for guests looking for a high\-end stay with a unique, urban feel. NYX Hotels is a part of the Leonardo Hotels portfolio. The Exchange Bar \& Restaurant is a vibrant, stylish, and high‑energy venue known for quality dining, exceptional service, and a lively atmosphere. We’re looking for an Experienced Waiter who brings confidence, warmth, and professionalism to every guest interaction. If you thrive in a fast‑paced restaurant environment and take pride in delivering outstanding service, this is the role for you. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ * Provide exceptional, attentive, and friendly table service throughout each shift. * Greet guests warmly, take accurate orders, and deliver dishes with confidence and care. * Maintain strong menu knowledge, including allergens, specials, and drink pairings. * Communicate clearly with the kitchen and bar teams to ensure seamless service. * Anticipate guest needs and resolve any concerns promptly and professionally. * Maintain a clean, organised, and polished service area at all times. * Support the team during busy operations and contribute to a positive, collaborative atmosphere. * Uphold high standards of presentation, accuracy, and hospitality. **Ideal Candidate** ------------------- * Previous experience as a waiter in a busy bar/restaurant environment. * Energetic, friendly, and passionate about guest service. * Confident communicator with excellent people skills. * Strong multitasking ability and calm under pressure. * Team‑oriented, reliable, and committed to high standards. * Professional appearance and positive attitude at all times. **Benefits** ------------ * Staff dining perks and hotel rate benefits (where applicable). * Meals on duty. * Recognition rewards and team celebrations. * Talent referral bonuses. * Wellbeing support and company benefits programme. * Opportunities for growth within the restaurant and across the wider business. **Available Ongoing Learning** ------------------------------ * GROWonline BELONG induction and development courses * Academy Technical Skills training * Team Coach programmes * “Come Join Us” Guest Care training * Opportunities to apply for internal development programmes, including: * Junior Chef Development Programme * Graduate Programme * Level 2 Apprenticeships in Hospitality **Note:** Learning opportunities may vary over time. Please speak with your line manager about available programmes. Some of the perks our Food \& Beverage Assistant could enjoy include:* Special rates on Leonardo Hotel rooms across the UK \& Europe * Company\-wide recognition scheme: you could earn vouchers to spend on a wide range of high street shops * Talent referral scheme * Thank You Week: from ice cream trucks to yoga classes and lots in between! * Wellbeing Calendar * Ongoing job\-related training programmes with clear paths for progression We’re committed to creating a workplace where every individual—regardless of background, identity, or lived experience—is welcomed, valued, and respected for who they are. Belonging means embracing our differences and ensuring everyone feels seen, heard, and empowered. Why come join us as a Food \& Beverage Assistant? At NYX Hotels, we look after our City Lovers just as well as we look after our guests. Once you’re part of the NYX team, you’re part of the Leonardo Hotels community of over 55 hotels across the UK \& Ireland. Our regular Learning Bites, “Come Join Us Guest Care” training and Wellbeing webinars all help your progress with us. With support on tap, top\-notch practical training and clear progression you’ll be on track for a brilliant future in Hospitality!

title: From Events to Career Progression – Get into Recruitment!
description: **From Events to Career Progression – Get into Recruitment!** * **You’ve proven you can handle pressure — now prove how far you can go** * **Stop resetting after every event — start building long\-term progression** * **Your next role shouldn’t be “another job” — it should be a step up** We’re Access, one of Ireland’s fastest\-growing recruitment companies — and every single leader in our business started in an entry\-level role . **Progression here isn’t a promise. It’s a pattern** . In events, you deliver, reset, and start again, with no clear long\-term progression. In recruitment, your determination builds over time: you’ll own relationships, develop your market, and progress based on performance *. Hannah joined us with no recruitment experience and was promoted to Director just last month, showing exactly what happens when ambition meets a structure designed for growth* . **What’s in it for you?** * **Clear promotion pathway** – you’ll always know what’s needed to reach the next level, giving you control over your progression * **Fast\-track progression** – you’ll be able to move quickly when you perform, without waiting years for opportunities * **Leadership opportunities** – you’ll have the chance to step into management roles and increase your impact * **Increasing responsibility and autonomy** – you’ll grow into a role where your decisions shape outcomes * **A career that builds over time** – you’ll stop starting from zero and start creating long\-term success **What we look for:** * 1 to 3 years of experience as an **Event Coordinator, Event Executive, Project Coordinator, Account Executive, or similar** * Experience working in fast\-paced, deadline\-driven environments * Strong stakeholder management and organisational skills * Ambition to progress and take on more responsibility **Additional Information:** Full\-time, office\-based role in Dublin City Centre, Monday to Friday. In your first 2 years, you can expect to earn an average of €64K\+ OTE, with accelerated progression based on performance. For more information, contact Ines Duarte confidentially at \+353 1 572 118 1 or email ines.duarte@accesshealthcare.ie

title: General Operative
description: **General Operative – Construction Manufacturing \| Kildare** **Competitive Salary \| Full Training Provided \| Career Growth Opportunities** Are you looking for a **hands\-on role** in a growing construction manufacturing company? Do you want a **stable, full\-time job** with great career prospects? Join **Select Fabrication** as a **General Operative** and be part of an innovative team in **Co. Kildare**! **Why You’ll Love This Job:** ✅ **Great Pay** – Competitive salary \& overtime available ✅ **No Experience? No Problem!** – Full training provided ✅ **Career Progression** – Long\-term opportunities in a growing company ✅ **Supportive Team** – Work in a friendly, safety\-first environment **About the Role:** As a **General Operative**, you will: ✔ Load and unload **customer orders**, ensuring accuracy with dockets ✔ Operate **sheet metal fabrication machines** (full training provided) ✔ Assist in **powder coating** to produce high\-quality finished products ✔ Follow **Health \& Safety (H\&S) procedures** at all times ✔ Work as part of a team to ensure smooth production **What You Need:** ✔ **A great work ethic** – We value reliable, hardworking team players ✔ **Attention to detail** – Accuracy is key when handling orders ✔ **Physically fit** – The role involves manual handling ✔ **A commitment to safety** – Full PPE and training provided ✔ **Experience in manufacturing/construction** is a plus (but not required!) **About Us:** **Select Fabrications** is a leading construction manufacturing company, specializing in **sheet metal fabrication**. We are expanding rapidly and looking for motivated individuals to join our **Co. Kildare** team. Be part of a company that values **growth, teamwork, and safety**. **Apply Today!** If you want a **stable, rewarding** job in a thriving industry, we want to hear from you! Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €16\.00\-€17\.50 per hour Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * On\-site parking Application question(s): * This role is based in Allenwood, Co. title: General Practitioner (GP)  with special interest in Palliative Medicine
description: Title: Hospice Doctor/General Practitioner (GP) with special interest in Palliative Medicine Location: LauraLynn House, Leopardstown, Dublin 18 Hours: Part\-time (18\.5 hrs per week). Consideration will also be given to applicants seeking full time (37 hours per week) Salary/Grade: 18\.5 hrs \- €82,505 gross rising to €83,330 gross from 01 Jun 2026 Equivalent Pro Rata Full\-time salary: €165,010 gross (37 hours) rising to €166,660 gross from 01 Jun 2026 Contract Type: Temporary and/or Permanent Job Closing Date: Tuesday 21st April 2026 **Introduction:** The GP will provide medical care in LauraLynn Children's Hospice ensuring the delivery of a high\-quality patient/service user centred service. Working with medical colleagues, The GP provides direct clinical care and all associated medical related activities, including participation in interdisciplinary meetings, family meetings and other relevant activities. The post holder will be required to liaise with other managers and staff in the LauraLynn Hospice service. The post holder will contribute to overall management of the organisation as well as input as appropriate to the development and implementation of organisational policies and procedures. **Benefits:** * Opportunity to work with a dynamic team focussed on supporting people to live their best possible life. * Free car parking * Pension scheme * Subsidised restuarant * Generous annual leave. * VHI group scheme * Bike to work scheme. * Active wellbeing supports including an in\-house occupational health service and a free 24/7/365 employee assistance programme * Excellent learning and development opportunities * Comprehensive orientation pack * Extensive in\-house person\-centred training programme for nursing, clinical and care staff. * Flexible working options * Access to membership of the HSSCU credit union. **Essential Criteria for the Role:** * MB BCH BAO or equivalent. * Full registration on the specialist division of the Medical Council of Ireland under the speciality of general practice or be eligible to apply to be included on this register, or * Current full registration on an alternative appropriate specialist division of the Medical Council of Ireland under an alternative specialism or eligible to be included on such register * MICGP or MRCPI paediatrics, part 1 or MRCPCH Theory exam. * Clinical experience in: Acute paediatrics either during general practice post graduate training and/or paediatric training. * Completion of at least 4 years post\-internship experience in paediatrics or general practice. * Demonstrate an understanding of the importance of multi\-agency working in the context of management of children with life\-limiting conditions. How to Apply: Applications for this role must be made below. Applications received via email will not be considered. For a copy of the job description and person specification contact our HR Department, E: hr@lauralynn.ie For informal enquiries please contact Dr Sozan Abdelrahman Consultant Paediatrician, on 01 2893151 or Email: sabdelrahman@lauralynn.ie **Should you need assistance or have any special needs when attending for interview, please contact any member of the HR team and reasonable accommodation will be arranged**

title: Graduate Programme 2026
description: **Windward are now accepting applications for their brand\-new Graduate Management Programme commencing in September 2026\.** The successful applicants will each be placed into a Graduate Manager role in one of our managed hotels across Ireland including ***Farnham Estate Spa \& Golf Resort, Aghadoe Heights Hotel \& Spa, Mount Wolseley Hotel Spa \& Golf Resort, The Diamond Coast Hotel, Connemara Coast Hotel and Harvey’s Point Lakeside Resort.*** **About us** Windward are a team of highly experienced hotel operators and investors with a hotel portfolio of approx. 2,000 keys\* in Ireland and Continental Europe. Founded in 2007, Windward is a leading hospitality management company, managing and operating hotels on behalf of their owners. **Requirements** * 3rd level degree in Hotel or Hospitality Management or related field of study * Willingness to relocate to location across Ireland * Passionate about hotels and the hospitality industry Applications are now open so apply today! **About The Role** **The Programme** Commencing in September 2026, the new Windward Graduate Management Programme is an 18\-month, rotational programme designed to equip graduates with the skills, knowledge and experience necessary to become future leaders in the hospitality industry by providing comprehensive training and hands\-on experience across various departments within our hotels. The programme is structured into six 3\-month rotations across different departments across: * Front Office * Reservations \& Revenue Management * Food \& Beverage * Housekeeping * Support Functions (Finance, HR, Sales \& Marketing) * Ops Management This rotational approach allows graduates to gain in\-depth experience and an understanding of each department's operations and how they interconnect to deliver exceptional guest experiences. In addition to the day\-to\-day experience, Graduate Managers will be enrolled on numerous additional qualifications to enhance their professional and personal development including Management Training Level 5, Train the Trainer and much more. With guidance from General Managers and HR, the programme will be underpinned by a curriculum of learning objectives that the graduate must achieve to ensure they are on the right track. Each graduate will also be assigned a mentor within the hotel to provide support and guidance. The overall objective of the programme is to create a pipeline to secure and train excellent future managers and leaders across the group. **Required Criteria** **Skills Needed** **About The Company** Since its inception in 2007, Windward Management Limited has built a reputation for excellence in hotel management. With headquarters in Dublin, the company provides tailored solutions for hotel owners, including operations, marketing, and financial management. Windward's team is driven by a passion for delivering exceptional guest experiences while ensuring each property achieves its full potential. Recognized for its dedication to innovation and operational success, the company is equally committed to investing in its people. Professionals joining Windward Management will become part of a supportive and dynamic environment, with abundant opportunities to grow and excel within a thriving sector. **Company Culture** At Windward Management, people are at the heart of success. The company nurtures a culture rooted in respect, collaboration, and a shared commitment to excellence. Employees are empowered to innovate and contribute to meaningful outcomes while enjoying a strong focus on work\-life balance. Windward fosters professional growth through tailored development opportunities, ensuring each team member can achieve their career aspirations. This positive, inclusive workplace values individual contributions, encourages open communication, and celebrates success collectively. For hospitality professionals seeking a rewarding career in an environment where their efforts truly matter, Windward Management offers an exceptional opportunity to thrive. **Company Benefits** Windward Management Limited understands the value of its people and offers a benefits package designed to support and reward its employees. Competitive salaries are complemented by a supportive work environment that prioritizes work\-life balance and career development. Team members benefit from opportunities to learn, grow, and advance in their careers, with access to mentoring and professional training programs. The company places a strong emphasis on employee well\-being, fostering a culture of mutual respect and collaboration. For candidates looking for a career that combines growth, fulfillment, and an opportunity to make an impact, Windward Management provides an ideal platform. **Salary** Not disclosed
